exp_title: C. floridanus major worker antennal RNA-seq
RNA-Seq of Camponotus floridanus: Major worker brain 3h following treatment with DMSO
design: Individual ant brains were dissected, rinsed in sterile Hanks balanced salt solution media (HBSS) and transferred to 1.5-ml microcentrifuge tubes containing 15 l of ice-cooled HBSS and snap-frozen in liquid nitrogen within 1 minute of dissection. Total RNA was purified from individual brains by trizol extraction, followed by DNase treatment using TURBO DNase (Invitrogen).  DNase-treated RNA was subsequently purified using RNase-free Agencourt AMPure XP beads (volume of beads:sample). Polyadenylated RNA was purified from total RNA using poly-A selection with on-bead fragmentation.  cDNA libraries were prepared the same day using the NEBNext Ultra II Directional RNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina.  PCR cycle number was determined empirically using qPCR side reactions performed on 10% of the purified adapter-ligated cDNA, but fell within 9-11 cycles for all samples, and all samples within a batch were amplified using the same number of PCR cycles.

Polyadenylated RNA was purified from total RNA using poly-A selection with on-bead fragmentation.  cDNA libraries were prepared the same day using the NEBNext Ultra II Directional RNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina.  PCR cycle number was determined empirically using qPCR side reactions performed on 10% of the purified adapter-ligated cDNA, but fell within 9-11 cycles for all samples, and all samples within a batch were amplified using the same number of PCR cycles.
